scenario: A SaaS cluster operator wants their tenant acme to be isolated from tenant globex. Workers should only pick up jobs for their tenant. A Java Job Worker uses Spring Zeebe. Which @JobWorker parameter handles tenant scoping?
question: Which @JobWorker parameter handles tenant scoping?

options.text: No tenant scoping is available in Job Workers; the application must implement its own filtering logic after receiving jobs. Relying on application-level filtering is fragile and brittle -- a bug in the filter could cause a worker to process another tenant's jobs, violating data isolation.

Set tenantId as a FEEL expression pointing to a process variable value. Process variables in Camunda 8 are instance-level data that changes with each process execution. Tenant identity is a broker-level and worker-level configuration concern, not an instance variable. Setting tenant isolation through a process variable expression would not restrict which jobs the worker activates from the broker job stream at the subscription level.

PROVIDED policy. The worker's job subscription is restricted to the specified tenant namespaces at the broker level, ensuring no cross-tenant job activation. Multiple tenant IDs can be specified as a comma-separated list or configured globally via YAML.

The Camunda Spring Boot Starter provides tenant-scoped job activation via the tenantIds attribute on @JobWorker annotations. Workers configured with tenantIds restrict their job polling to specific tenant namespaces, ensuring data isolation across tenant boundaries. Multi-tenancy is a first-class feature in Camunda 8 Self-Managed, not an experimental add-on, and tenant isolation is enforced at the job-activation layer by Zeebe.

a.text: Incorrect. Per apis-tools/camunda-spring-boot-starter/configuration.md:884-887, the @JobWorker annotation supports the tenantIds attribute: @JobWorker(tenantIds="myOtherTenant"). Default policy is TenantFilter.PROVIDED (worker retrieves jobs only for explicitly configured tenant IDs -- :872). Global worker defaults via camunda.client.worker.defaults.tenant-ids (YAML, lines 877-880). Note: spring-zeebe-sdk is being removed in 8.10 (Pattern #18) -- use camunda-spring-boot-starter instead.

b.text: Incorrect. Tenant filtering at app level is brittle; broker-side scoping is authoritative.

c.text: Correct. Process variables are instance-level; tenant is broker/job-level.

explanation: Process variables are instance-level; tenant is broker/job-level.
